channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Essendon defender refutes claims of laziness amid losing streak
item.description: Essendon defender Ben McKay has pushed back against criticism from former player Matthew Lloyd, insisting the Bombers are far from lazy despite their current on-field struggles. The team recently suff...

item.title: TSG urges Tinubu’s appointees to support party members without government roles
item.description: The Tinubu Support Group (TSG) don call on all government appointees to remember and support their fellow party members wey never get position yet. Dr Umar Tanko Yakasai, the Director-General of TSG, ...

item.title: Taliban report heavy civilian casualties after airstrike on Kabul drug rehabilitation hospital
item.description: At least 400 people were reported killed and 250 injured in an airstrike on the Omid drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan, according to a spokesman for the Afghan Taliban government. The...

item.title: Torcross villagers express disappointment after exclusion from £1.4bn government coastal and flood defence funding
item.description: Residents of Torcross and nearby Beesands in South Devon are facing significant challenges following severe damage caused by Storm Imogen in February 2026. The storm washed away a 200-metre section of...

item.title: 14 detained in Varanasi over Iftar on Ganga after complaint regarding religious sentiments
item.description: In Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, fourteen individuals were taken into custody following allegations of defiling a place of worship and hurting religious feelings. The arrests followed the viral circulation...

item.title: Magufuli’s Legacy Reflected in Tanzanians’ Work Ethic and Community Spirit
item.description: Jesca John Magufuli, daughter of the late President John Pombe Magufuli, highlighted that her father’s real legacy is not only in the roads, schools, and railways built under his leadership, but in th...

item.title: LTFRB Increases TNVS Base Fare to Help Drivers Cope with Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Transport organizations in the Philippines have welcomed the recent fare adjustments announced by the Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) for transport network vehicle service...

item.title: Free Health Screening for Aviation Staff to Boost Safety Standards
item.description: Aviation staff in Tanzania soon go get free health check-ups under new programme designed to improve safety by spotting medical problems early that fit affect their work performance. The screenings, a...

item.title: Few Newfoundland and Labrador Restaurants Access Provincial Loan Guarantee Program Amid Rising Costs
item.description: Last spring, the Newfoundland and Labrador government introduced a $10-million loan guarantee program for restaurants, aiming to help businesses manage debt and finance necessary equipment or repairs....

item.title: Joe Kent Resigns as NCTC Director Citing Opposition to U.S. Military Action in Iran
item.description: Joe Kent, the Director of the National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC) appointed by former President Donald Trump, has resigned, citing fundamental disagreement with the ongoing U.S. military campaign ...

item.title: Rooftop Fire in Midtown Manhattan Disrupts St. Patrick’s Day Celebrations
item.description: On Tuesday, March 17, 2026, a fire broke out on the roof of a building located at 6 E 43rd Street in Midtown Manhattan, coinciding with St. Patrick's Day festivities in New York City. The FDNY confirm...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Crossword Challenge for 18 March 2026
item.description: The article titled 'Superquiz, Wednesday, March 18' is a brief daily feature from The Sydney Morning Herald, inviting readers to participate in the publication’s popular Superquiz and Mini Crossword c...

item.title: Kenya Floods Claim 71 Lives as Heavy Rains Persist
item.description: As of 17 March 2026, Kenya has recorded 71 deaths due to ongoing floods, with Nairobi bearing the brunt at 36 fatalities. Other affected regions include Eastern (19), Rift Valley (8), Nyanza (4), Coas...

item.title: Tanzania outlines eight strategies to boost minority government-owned companies for Vision 2050
item.description: The Tanzanian government has rolled out eight key directives focused on improving the performance of companies where it owns minority shares. This move is part of the broader plan to grow Tanzania’s e...

item.title: South Africa faces industrial gas shortage by 2028 with potential 70,000 job losses
item.description: South Africa is facing a looming industrial gas crisis that could severely affect its economy and employment. Many local factories, including steel mills, glass plants, breweries, and food manufacture...

item.title: Seven Foreign Nationals Arrested in India for Alleged Terror Conspiracy
item.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has taken into custody seven foreign nationals over allegations of conspiring to commit terrorist acts against India. Among those arrested, six are Ukrainians a...

item.title: Senate Secretariat to cut expenditures under Gilani’s austerity plan
item.description: Senate Chairman Yousuf Raza Gilani on Tuesday directed strict austerity measures in the Senate Secretariat to save around Rs700-750 million for the current fiscal year. In a high-level meeting, Gilani...

item.title: Lulu claims Maurice Gibb may have fathered a child during their marriage
item.description: Scottish singer Lulu, aged 77, has revealed on The Louis Theroux Podcast that she has recently discovered that her late ex-husband Maurice Gibb, of the Bee Gees, may have fathered a son during their s...

item.title: Dar highlights dialogue and diplomacy as key to regional stability during Iran envoy meeting
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has underlined that dialogue and diplomacy are the only practical ways to resolve conflicts and ensure lasting peace in the region. He made these r...

item.title: Defense Secretary Teodoro dismisses China’s 1990 Scarborough Reef document as non-binding internal political tactic
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. rejected a 1990 document circulated by the Chinese Embassy in Manila concerning Scarborough Reef (also known as Bajo de Masinloc) in the West Philippine Sea. He ...
